text.title: Overlap Zone Governance and Agent Constraints
text.summary: Defines DRI assignment rules for PM-vs-X overlap zones, agent role constraints, the quarterly audit cadence, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints.

rule.statement: Name exactly one human PM DRI for each overlap zone (PM/design, PM/growth, PM/engineer, PM/data, PM/eval); agents fill the zone but only the PM closes decisions.
rule.rationale: A zone without a DRI becomes a place where decisions stall instead of accelerate — the Venn overlap is only productive when the convergence point is a named human with decision authority.

rule.statement: Document the DRI assignment in the PM job description, not in tribal knowledge; it must survive PM turnover.
rule.rationale: Undocumented DRI assignments disappear when the PM leaves; the next PM inherits ambiguity and re-creates the handoff gaps the Venn model was designed to eliminate.

rule.statement: Constrain agent personas via tool allow-lists: faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent must not have Edit permissions on source files; PM-vs-engineer overlap must not collapse at the file level.
rule.rationale: An agent given the PM JD plus repo access will silently rewrite code in the PM-vs-engineer overlap zone; tool allow-lists are the only reliable constraint, not prompt instructions.

rule.statement: Use faion-brainstorm to fan out persona-flavored agents (PM-agent, designer-agent, growth-agent, eng-agent) on the same problem in parallel, then converge in a PM-DRI session.
rule.rationale: Parallel role-persona agents produce contradictions; without a human PM-DRI to converge, the PRD ships the loudest agent's recommendation — the diverge-converge structure forces explicit resolution.

antipattern.description: Agent-driven PM evaluations rating PM data fluency from PRDs used as performance inputs.
antipattern.reason: LLMs are biased toward verbose writers and score calibration against PRD length, not insight; use as a discussion seed only, never as a performance or leveling input.

section.title: Quarterly Scope Audit

rule.statement: Re-audit PM scope quarterly with faion-improver; blurred PM roles drift back to delivery-brokering under deadline pressure.
rule.rationale: Scope drift is invisible without measurement; the quarterly audit forces the conversation about where the PM actually spent time vs. the Venn target, using commit-author and ticket-assignee data as ground truth.

rule.statement: Use commit-author and Linear/Jira-assignee distribution as the truth signal for current PM scope; self-reported scope in surveys is systematically wrong by approximately 30%.
rule.rationale: PMs systematically under-report time spent on delivery brokering and over-report time on discovery; objective data from git and trackers corrects this bias.

section.reference: Emit PM ticket-type distribution from Linear (discovery vs. delivery vs. handoff)

list.item: PM-DRI assignment per overlap zone — before any agent pair runs on a shared problem.
Approval of any PM JD rewrite generated by an agent.
Explicit sign-off on PM:Engineer ratio change before any hiring action.
PM review of every eval set drafted by an agent before it enters a test suite.
